summary: This is an informal camera gear review, not a technical software post. Fowler reviews the Sony a6000 mirrorless camera paired with the Sony-Zeiss 16-70mm lens, explaining his switch from a Canon DSLR system primarily motivated by weight concerns. He finds the combination satisfying for his travel photography needs while acknowledging the Canon still has advantages for certain situations like informal portraits and wildlife photography.
key_points: Weight was the primary motivation for switching from DSLR to mirrorless
The combination of a tilting LCD, good viewfinder, and appropriate lens was essential criteria
Camera quality is third on the list of factors for good photography, behind location and composition
The Sony has become his principal serious camera for trips
He still keeps his Canon system for specific use cases like informal portraits and when weight is not a concern
money_quotes: I consider that the quality of your camera is, at best, third on the list of factors of getting a good photograph.
Dawn in the Grand Tetons is always going to trump a cloudy lunchtime in Walsall.
A good photographer will get better pictures with 'lesser' equipment than a poor one.
Any camera I'm likely to buy is going to be a better camera than I am a photographer.
key_insight: Equipment matters less than being in the right place and having good compositional skills, so optimize for what helps you actually take more photos.
